zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud Giffard於1207年出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾布利普斯費爾德時，她的父親Elias Giffard28歲，母親Isabel Musard24歲。她和Godfrey Scudamore至少有5個兒子。她去世於1227年在英國英格蘭賀福爾肯丘其享年20歲。

nameMeanings.name: Giffard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: (i) relationship name from the Continental Germanic personal name Gifard. (ii) nickname, perhaps sometimes from Old French giffard ‘chubby-cheeked, bloated’.

variants.name: Jeffords
meanings: English: variant of Gifford , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Guiver
meanings: English: (i) nickname, apparently from Middle English giver ‘giver, donor, bestower’. (ii) variant of Giffard with voicing of /f/ to /v/ and loss of final -d.

variants.name: Gafford
meanings: English (southeastern): assimilated form of Gatford, a name of various origins. In Sussex and Surrey it is perhaps a variant of the habitational name Catford from a place so called in Somerset. Gatford is also from the habitational names Gateford (in Worksop, Nottinghamshire) or Gateforth (near Selby, Yorkshire), both formed with Old English gāt ‘goat’ + ford ‘ford’, influenced by Old Norse geit ‘goat’.

Probably also an altered form of German Gaffert: habitational name from Gaffert near Köslin, Brandenburg, or from a personal name formed with Middle High German gate ‘fellow, companion’. The surname Gaffert is very rare in Germany.

English: perhaps an altered form of Gatewood , an occupational name for a ‘goatherd’ (Middle English gate-werde, Old English gātweard) or for a ‘gate keeper’ (Middle English gateward, Old English ʒeatweard, dialect gātward).

variants.name: Gifford
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Giffard, either from the Middle English personal name Gifard, derived from an Old French name of this form deriving from ancient Germanic geb- ‘give, gift’ + hard- ‘hardy, brave’, or perhaps sometimes a nickname from Old French gif(f)ard ‘chubby-cheeked, bloated’.

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud Giffard was born in 1207, in Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, England, her father, Elias Giffard, was 28 and her mother, Isabel Musard, was 24. She had at least 5 sons with Godfrey Scudamore. She died in 1227, in Kentchurch, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 20.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud Giffard est née en 1207, à Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, son père, Elias Giffard, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Isabel Musard, avait 24 ans. Elle a eu au moins 5 fils avec Godfrey Scudamore. Elle est décédée en 1227, à Kentchurch, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 20 ans.

Sie hatte mit Godfrey Scudamore mindestens 5 Söhne. Sie starb 1227 im Alter von 20 Jahren in Kentchurch, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Maud Giffard è nata il nel 1207 nel Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, England, sua padre, Elias Giffard, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Isabel Musard, aveva 24 anni. Lei avuto almeno 5 figli con Godfrey Scudamore. Morì nel 1227, nel Kentchurch, Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 20 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Maud Giffard(이)가 1207년 영국 그루스스터실르 브림프스필드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Elias Giffard(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel Musard(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 Godfrey Scudamore(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1227년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 켄트c후르치에서 20세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud Giffard родилась в 1207 г. в местности бримпсфиелд, глоусестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени Elias Giffard было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel Musard, 24. У неё было по крайней мере 5 сыновей от Godfrey Scudamore. Она умерла в 1227 г. в местности кэнчурч, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 20 лет.
